content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 21, 1990


R. Clifford Lober
Attorney at Law
Five Financial Plaza, Suite 120
Napa, CA  94558

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     File No. A-90-135

Dear Mr. Lober:

     You have requested advice concerning the NAVCO Pacific
Development Corporation's duties under the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

     Is the NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation an "agency"
under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION

     The NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation is a local
government agency and is subject to the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.

FACTS

     The Napa Valley Community College District (College) owns
surplus real property which it desires to develop.  The
administration for the College considered leasing the property to
a developer.  It was suggested to the College that it would be
more efficient to lease the property to a nonprofit corporation
who would in turn sublease it to a developer, rather than lease to
a developer directly.  The NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation
(NAVCO) was formed specifically for this purpose.

_________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Page Two
March 21, 1990


     NAVCO consists of five directors who were independently
appointed by a superior court judge. At present, there are no
College employees or trustees as members of NAVCO. The College is
considering assuming more control by requesting an increase in the
number of directors so that it could appoint two of its members.
The College does maintain control over the type of use NAVCO may
obtain for the land as well as the right to review plans for
structures on the property.

     It is the College's intention that the land be used to
accommodate the development of a hotel and there is a developer
interested in pursuing such a project. Under the proposal, during
interested in pursuing such a project. Under the proposal, during
the first four years of operation, the developer will reimburse
NAVCO for the rental amounts owed to the College and pay NAVCO's
operating expenses. The developer guarantees to pay one million
dollars per year income after the fourth operating year of the
hotel. All of the income derived from the hotel would go to the
College as either income or as a gift.

     NAVCO has been granted 501(c)(3) tax exempt status by the
Internal Revenue Service, which is a status enjoyed similarly by
other public agencies. The activities of NAVCO are limited by the
tax exemption to higher education in general and Napa Valley
Community College specifically.


                            ANALYSIS

     "Local government agency" is defined in Section 82041 as:
     ...a county, city or district of any kind including
     school district, or any other local or regional
     political subdivision, or any department, division,
     bureau, office, board, commission or other agency
     of the foregoing.

     In determining whether a particular entity is a private
entity or a local government agency, the Commission in its
Siegel27 Opinion, stated that certain criteria should be
considered. These criteria are:

     (1) Whether the impetus for formation of the
     corporation originated with a government agency;

     (2) Whether it is substantially funded by, or its
     primary source of funds is, a government agency;

     (3) Whether one of the principal purposes for which it
     is formed is to provide services or undertake
     obligations which public agencies are legally authorized
     to and which, in fact, they traditionally have
     performed; and


27  In re Siegel (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 62, copy enclosed.

Page Three
March 21, 1990


     (4)  Whether the corporation is treated as a public
entity by other statutory provisions.

Impetus for Formation

     It is your contention that the impetus for formation was the
suggestion by a third party to form the nonprofit corporation, not
with the College.  We disagree.  A third party may have initiated
the idea, but had it not been for the concurrence of the College,
there would have been no need to form NAVCO.  You have indicated
that the activities of NAVCO are limited by the tax exemption to
higher education in general and Napa Valley Community College
specifically.  It appears that NAVCO's sole purpose for existence
is its leasing arrangement with the College.

Funded by a Government Agency

     The College has leased 45 acres of land to NAVCO for $1,158
per acre per year.  A proposed developer has agreed to reimburse
NAVCO for that rental amount and to pay any operating expenses.
After the proposed hotel has been in operation for four years, the
developer guarantees to pay one million dollars per year of income
to the College, first as rent as stated above, and the balance to
the College as a gift.

     All of the funding for NAVCO will be derived from revenues
generated from the property owned by the College, a government
agency.  Without that government owned property, there would be no
source of revenues.  The second criterion of Siegel is met.

Service Which Public Agencies Traditionally Perform

     You have indicated that NAVCO is providing a service which
the College could perform.  We agree with this.  The third
criterion of Siegel is met.

Treated as a Public Entity by Other Statutes

     NAVCO, as a nonprofit public benefit corporation, enjoys
certain tax benefits similar to other public agencies.  You have
also indicated that NAVCO could be subject to the Brown Act.  The
fourth criterion of Siegel is met as well.

     Accordingly, we conclude that NAVCO is a local government
agency within the meaning of Section 82041 and should be covered
by a conflict of interest code.  The pending expansion of the
board from five to seven members has no bearing on this
determination.

Page Four
March 21, 1990


I have enclosed for your review a copy of Commission
Regulation 18750.1. This regulation describes the procedures
agencies are to follow when adopting or amending a conflict of
interest code. Also enclosed are fact sheets and a sample of
another code to assist you.

Please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any
questions, or if I can be of further assistance.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

KED/JET/jt
Enclosures

sections.question: Is the NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation an "agency" under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion: The NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation is a local government agency and is subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.facts:
The Napa Valley Community College District (College) owns surplus real property which it desires to develop. The administration for the College considered leasing the property to a developer. It was suggested to the College that it would be more efficient to lease the property to a nonprofit corporation who would in turn sublease it to a developer, rather than lease to a developer directly. The NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation (NAVCO) was formed specifically for this purpose.

1, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

NAVCO consists of five directors who were independently appointed by a superior court judge. At present, there are no College employees or trustees as members of NAVCO. The College is considering assuming more control by requesting an increase in the number of directors so that it could appoint two of its members. The College does maintain control over the type of use NAVCO may obtain for the land as well as the right to review plans for structures on the property.

It is the College's intention that the land be used to accommodate the development of a hotel and there is a developer interested in pursuing such a project. Under the proposal, during the first four years of operation, the developer will reimburse NAVCO for the rental amounts owed to the college and pay NAVCO's operating expenses. The developer guarantees to pay one million dollars per year income after the fourth operating year of the hotel. All of the income derived from the hotel would go to the College as either income or as a gift.

NAVCO has been granted 501(c)(3) tax exempt status by the Internal Revenue Service, which is a status enjoyed similarly by other public agencies. The activities of NAVCO are limited by the tax exemption to higher education in general and Napa Valley Community College specifically.
sections.analysis:
"Local government agency" is defined in Section 82041 as:
...a county, city or district of any kind including school district, or any other local or regional political subdivision, or any department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing.

In determining whether a particular entity is a private entity or a local government agency, the Commission in its Siegel2/ Opinion, stated that certain criteria should be considered. These criteria are:

(1) Whether the impetus for formation of the corporation originated with a government agency;

(2) Whether it is substantially funded by, or its primary source of funds is, a government agency;

(3) Whether one of the principal purposes for which it is formed is to provide services or undertake obligations which public agencies are legally authorized to perform and which, in fact, they traditionally have performed; and

2/ In re Siegel (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 62, copy enclosed.

(4) Whether the corporation is treated as a public entity by other statutory provisions.

Impetus for Formation

It is your contention that the impetus for formation was the suggestion by a third party to form the nonprofit corporation, not with the College. We disagree. A third party may have initiated the idea, but had it not been for the concurrence of the College, there would have been no need to form NAVCO. You have indicated that the activities of NAVCO are limited by the tax exemption to higher education in general and Napa Valley Community College specifically. It appears that NAVCO's sole purpose for existence is its leasing arrangement with the College.

Funded by a Government Agency

The College has leased 45 acres of land to NAVCO for $1,158 per acre per year. A proposed developer has agreed to reimburse NAVCO for that rental amount and to pay any operating expenses. After the proposed hotel has been in operation for four years, the developer guarantees to pay one million dollars per year of income to the College, first as rent as stated above, and the balance to the College as a gift.

All of the funding for NAVCO will be derived from revenues generated from the property owned by the College, a government agency. Without that government owned property, there would be no source of revenues. The second criterion of Siegel is met.

Service Which Public Agencies Traditionally Perform

You have indicated that NAVCO is providing a service which the College could perform. We agree with this. The third criterion of Siegel is met.

Treated as a Public Entity by Other Statutes

NAVCO, as a nonprofit public benefit corporation, enjoys certain tax benefits similar to other public agencies. You have also indicated that NAVCO could be subject to the Brown Act. The fourth criterion of Siegel is met as well.

Accordingly, we conclude that NAVCO is a local government agency within the meaning of Section 82041 and should be covered by a conflict of interest code. The pending expansion of the board from five to seven members has no bearing on this determination.

March 21, 1990

I have enclosed for your review a copy of Commission Regulation 18750.1. This regulation describes the procedures agencies are to follow when adopting or amending a conflict of interest code. Also enclosed are fact sheets and a sample of another code to assist you.

Please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any questions, or if I can be of further assistance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation an "agency" under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The NAVCO Pacific Development Corporation is a local government agency and is subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
